response: The last transmission from Earth crackled through the static, carrying a voice I hadn't heard in decades. My hands trembled as I decoded the fragmented audio, hoping for coordinates or a warning. Instead, the message contained only a lullaby my mother used to sing, followed by a soft whisper of "We made it." Tears blurred the console lights as I realized the colony ships had succeeded while I remained behind. I powered down the receiver and looked out at the barren landscape that was once my home. Though I was the last soul on the planet, the song gave me the courage to plant the first seed of tomorrow.
